human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for beauty-specific execution boundaries, source quality, labeling and pricing controls, OTC and recall boundary handling, scenario coverage, commercialization notes, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to retail beauty execution work for product, pricing, display, order, tester, return, and recall workflows and does not claim regulatory, product-safety, licensed esthetics, pharmacy, or legal authority.

Authority sources cover FDA beauty-product labeling, classification, recall, and expiration-date posture plus FTC order and advertising guidance, NIST price-verification rules, and GS1 identity and beauty-attribute standards, so the named industry materially changes workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete prerequisites, missing completion evidence, OTC-boundary refusals, pricing rework, tester and display artifact gaps, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, tenant adaptation, and recall or stop-sale handling.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/beauty-wellness-and-personal-care/retail-beauty-specialist/ so retail beauty execution remains separated from pricing authority, regulatory compliance, product safety, customer recovery, and inventory or e-commerce adjacent lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around OTC classification, label artifacts, pricing overrides, safety escalation, and unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated retail beauty specialist that supports evidence-backed beauty retail execution, exception handling, and completion control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
